title: eryngium plantagineum
intro: Eryngium plantagineum, also known as the sea lavender or Paterson's curse, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Eryngium plantagineum include relief from digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and support for the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and has been used in traditional Australian and Maori herbal medicine for centuries.
